name: MCP Prompt Server (Go)
what_it_does: Go-based MCP server for managing and serving reusable prompt templates by smallnest — a prolific Go developer. Enables AI agents to load, query, and use stored prompt templates — supporting prompt engineering workflows, prompt versioning, and systematic prompt management for AI development teams.
use_cases: Loading and applying prompt templates from AI agent workflows
Managing prompt libraries for consistent agent behavior across workflows
Versioning and iterating on prompts from prompt engineering agents
Sharing prompt templates across team members and agent deployments
not_for: Single-use prompts that don't need to be reused or versioned
Teams using dedicated prompt management platforms (LangSmith, Portkey)
Simple completions without prompt template needs
best_when: An AI development team wants to manage prompt templates systematically — versioning prompts, sharing them across agents, and maintaining a prompt library for consistent AI behavior.
avoid_when: You use dedicated prompt management platforms. This is a lightweight local prompt store — evaluate whether you need the full features of platforms like LangSmith.

auth.notes: Local prompt store — no authentication required.

known_agent_gotchas: Prompt templates stored locally — not synced across team members without additional setup
smallnest is a well-regarded Go developer (rpcx, etc.) — Go implementation quality is likely solid
Prompt versioning strategy must be defined by user — server stores but doesn't enforce versions
34 stars is modest but Go quality signal from smallnest is strong
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local prompt storage. No credentials. Prompt templates stay local. Low security risk.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local storage. No credentials. Prompts stay local. Low risk.
